opp.ais_why:
• On Jan 7 2026, Vinoth Kumar (Nissan ME, Head of Procurement) said in the discovery call (note from Dan Quinn): 'Key requirements include full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence.' He continued: 'the system will give me an indication that based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ for this particular sourcing so that you can benefit out of the new technology or the cost.' Buyer explicitly naming AI-driven sourcing as a mandatory requirement.
• Per the 6 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Have paused the project due to the war... EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s)... next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis.' Vinoth wants a live custom demo built on his own process flow - signaling deep solution-led evaluation.
• High confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (nissan-me.ae domain), Jan 7 transcript with 114 notes including direct Vinoth quotes naming AI-driven sourcing and market intelligence integration. Product scope includes Merlin + Merlin Intake + Agentic AI + ANA. Project is paused due to war but the AI vision was articulated by the buyer pre-pause - AI Hungry stands.

metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated operational pain around manual procurement processes and lack of market intelligence integration, but no quantified business case has been developed. On the 08 January 2026 discovery call Vinoth Kumar (Dy Manager Purchase) and colleagues described current-state pain and expressed interest in automating PR-to-PO-to-invoicing workflows, but did not quantify cycle time, error rates, headcount impact, or savings targets. On 20 January 2026 Vinoth stated it is early stages and they lack the data to complete ROI estimation tables, requesting a high-level estimate instead. The Next Step field records on 06 April 2026 that the buyer is interested in a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) to be built out at a face-to-face demo session. No quantified metrics (cost savings, FTE reduction, cycle-time improvement) have been documented. The absence of a quantified value case at this stage (177 days in, 59 days to close) is a critical gap and a key reason the forecast is indefensible. Quantifying the business case is the immediate next move.

champion.narrative: No champion has been developed. Vinoth Kumar (Dy Manager Purchase, Decision Maker contact role) is the most engaged buyer contact and led the 08 January 2026 discovery call, but there is no evidence of champion behavior (internal advocacy, sharing political landscape, driving our agenda, or providing access to the Economic Buyer or HQ decision-makers). Vinoth stated on 08 January that the discovery was positively received and expressed interest in a live demo, but since the 06 April 2026 war-related pause there has been no buyer-initiated re-engagement. The last buyer contact was the 17 April 2026 call (77 days ago). Salonee Karnik (Purchasing Section Manager, Influencer contact role) was referenced in Next Step entries in January 2026 as the contact to provide demo date options, but no evidence of ongoing engagement or advocacy. The Next Step field on 06 April 2026 records that EMEA HQ may override the local team's solution choice, and neither Vinoth nor Salonee has provided visibility into HQ decision-makers or offered to facilitate introductions, which a true champion would do. With 77 days of buyer silence, no EB access, and HQ override risk unmitigated, the champion strength is weak and at high risk. Securing or rebuilding a champion is a top-priority move.

competition.narrative: Competing against SAP Ariba (confirmed) and facing a high-probability do-nothing / HQ-override scenario. The Competitors__c field lists SAP Ariba and Others. On the 08 January 2026 discovery call Vinoth Kumar and colleagues positively received the Zycus pitch and flagged market intelligence integration (Beroe vs SmartCube) as the only functional gap, suggesting Zycus is a strong functional fit. However, the Next Step field on 06 April 2026 records the critical competitive threat: EMEA headquarters may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be Zycus (deployed in US and Australia) or an unnamed alternative, bypassing the local Middle East team's evaluation. This HQ-override risk is the single highest competitive threat and has not been navigated. If HQ mandates Zycus, we win by default; if HQ mandates a competitor (SAP Ariba or other), the local evaluation is moot. The wedge is our existing Nissan footprint (US/Australia deployments) and the Merlin/Agentic AI differentiation, but we have not engaged HQ decision-makers to activate that wedge. With 77 days of buyer silence and 59 days to close, the risk is that the buyer (and Zycus) are sidelined by an HQ decision we are not part of. The immediate move is to map and engage the relevant stakeholder decision-makers and position our existing Nissan deployments as the low-risk, fast-path choice.

The pain is confirmed but not yet quantified or escalated to economic-buyer level.
